political.s: Boston Beer made $139,062 in 2024 political contributions from employees/owners and spent $120,000 on federal lobbying. The company operates in the heavily regulated alcohol industry and participates in trade association political advocacy through the Beer Institute and related groups.

charity.s: Boston Beer contributed over $2.34 million in 2021 to support Samuel Adams Brewing the American Dream (a program supporting food and beverage entrepreneurs), Dogfish Head's Beer & Benevolence program, and other community initiatives. The company launched Boston Beer Volunteers to engage employees with local nonprofits.
charity.focus: Entrepreneurship support, community nonprofits, food/beverage industry development

environment.s: Boston Beer released its inaugural ESG report in 2022 and has continued annual reporting. The company has set sustainability goals around energy, water, and waste reduction, engaged with CDP's Climate Change Questionnaire, and supports initiatives like regenerative agriculture in products such as Dogfish Head's Re-Gen-Ale.

labor.s: Boston Beer reported 2,740 employees as of May 2026. The company achieved 95% of bonus targets in recent years, provided 43,600 hours of employee learning and development in 2021, and promoted 430+ employees. Employee compensation ratings on Glassdoor were 3.7/5 stars with salaries ranging from approximately $50k to $158k for most roles.

dei.s: Boston Beer scored 70% on the Human Rights Campaign Corporate Equality Index in 2021 and continues active DEI programs including the promotion of salary pay ranges for transparency, expansion of Inclusion and Belonging curriculum, and support for LGBTQ+ initiatives including the Love Conquers Ale partnership with GLAAD. The company hired a full-time DEI leader in October 2020.

animals.s: Not applicable to beer brewing company.

privacy.s: Limited public information available regarding Boston Beer's data privacy practices or policies.

execPay.s: In 2023, former CEO David Burwick earned $5.9 million in total compensation. For 2024-2025, executives received 3-8% salary increases with performance-based bonuses tied to depletions, EBIT, and cost-savings metrics. Founder Jim Koch declined salary increases and now serves as CEO. Average employee salary is approximately $105k versus executive compensation in the $600k-$900k+ range.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
